sets.passage: For many people, the joy of travel begins long before the actual journey starts. The mere act of planning a trip can lift the spirit and (1)____ the imagination in wonderful ways. Studying maps, reading guidebooks, and picturing distant streets create a pleasant sense of (2)____ that quietly carries them through the dullest of workdays. Once they finally arrive, every ordinary detail suddenly feels fresh and (3)____, from the smell of unfamiliar food to the strange rhythm of a foreign language overheard on a crowded train. Travel gently forces us to step outside our daily (4)____ and observe the world with renewed curiosity. We begin to notice small things we would normally ignore at home, and tiny surprises become genuine (5)____. Meeting strangers who live very differently can quietly (6)____ our assumptions about what a good and meaningful life should actually look like. Such unexpected encounters often leave a (7)____ impression that stays with us for many years afterward. Of course, not every moment is smooth or pleasant; delayed trains and lost luggage can quickly (8)____ even the most patient and easygoing traveller. Yet these very difficulties frequently turn into the funniest stories we proudly tell our friends later on. Perhaps the deepest reward of travel is the way it (9)____ us to return home and see our own familiar city through completely new eyes. In the end, the true value of any trip cannot be measured by photographs or cheap souvenirs alone, but rather by the quiet inner (10)____ it slowly produces within us over time.
sets.explain_zh: (1)stimulate 激发，与 lift 并列的及物动词原形，接宾语 the imagination。(2)anticipation 期待，pleasant sense of ___，名词。(3)vivid 生动鲜明的，feels fresh and ___，形容词作表语。(4)routines 日常惯例，daily ___，名词复数，daily routines 固定搭配。(5)delights 乐事，surprises become genuine ___，名词复数作表语。(6)challenge 质疑/挑战，___ our assumptions，及物动词原形。(7)lasting 持久的，a ___ impression，形容词修饰 impression。(8)frustrate 使沮丧，quickly ___ traveller，及物动词原形接人。(9)encourages 促使，the way it ___ us to return，第三人称单数动词，encourage sb to do。(10)transformation 转变/升华，quiet inner ___，名词中心词。干扰项：decorate(10) 装饰，无及物动词槽匹配语义；briefly(11) 简短地，副词无修饰对象，不匹配任何空格；narrow(12) 狭窄的，无合适形容词槽；loudly(13) 副词无修饰对象；honest(14) 诚实的，无形容词槽匹配。

sets.passage: Minimalism is often misunderstood as a cold obsession with empty rooms, but its real (1)____ is freedom rather than emptiness. People who begin decluttering their homes soon discover that owning far less can dramatically (2)____ the mental noise that fills a crowded space. Every unused object silently (3)____ for a little of our attention, reminding us of decisions we never quite made and money we perhaps wasted. By removing these endless reminders, we slowly create both physical and emotional (4)____ to breathe and think clearly. The process is rarely easy at first, however. Many of us feel a strange (5)____ to keep things simply because we once paid for them or received them as gifts from people we love. Letting go can feel almost (6)____, as though we were quietly betraying our own past selves. Yet those who persist usually describe a surprising sense of (7)____ afterward, as if a heavy weight had finally been lifted from their shoulders. A tidy, deliberate space tends to (8)____ calmer thinking and clearer daily priorities. It becomes much easier to focus on what genuinely matters when the surroundings are no longer (9)____ and distracting. Importantly, minimalism is never about counting possessions or following strict rules; it is instead a flexible personal (10)____ that each individual adapts to their own particular life. Some keep only a single backpack of belongings, while others simply tidy one small drawer at a time, and both honest approaches are equally valid. What unites every minimalist is a quiet conviction that a lighter, calmer home can support a far richer and more attentive way of living each day.
sets.explain_zh: (1)goal 目标，its real ___ is freedom，名词作主语。(2)reduce 减少，dramatically ___ the noise，及物动词原形。(3)demands 索求，object silently ___ for attention，第三人称单数动词，demand for 搭配。(4)room 空间/余地（不可数），create physical and emotional ___，名词。(5)reluctance 不情愿，feel a strange ___ to keep，名词后接不定式。(6)disloyal 不忠的，feel almost ___，形容词表语。(7)relief 解脱，a sense of ___，名词。(8)encourage 促进，tends to ___ calmer thinking，原形动词。(9)chaotic 混乱的，no longer ___ and distracting，形容词表语。(10)philosophy 理念，flexible personal ___，名词中心词。干扰项：donate(10) 捐赠，原形动词槽语义不合（非“鼓励思考”类）；purchase(11) 购买，无名词/动词槽匹配；loudly(12) 副词无修饰对象；ancient(13) 古老的语义不合；patient(14) 有耐心的，无合适形容词槽（(6)(9) 语义唯一）。

sets.passage: The global fashion industry has long been criticised for its enormous environmental cost, and sustainable fashion has gradually emerged as a serious (1)____ to that growing problem. Cheap, fast-changing clothing constantly encourages shoppers to buy far more than they truly need, then carelessly (2)____ perfectly wearable garments after only a few wears. Mountains of textile waste now (3)____ landfills all around the world, slowly releasing harmful chemicals into the ground as they decay. Sustainable brands try hard to (4)____ this damage by using organic fibres, recycled materials, and natural dyes that are far less (5)____ to rivers and soil. They also pay much closer attention to the people who actually make the clothes, ensuring fair wages and (6)____ working conditions in factories that are too often hidden from public view. For ordinary consumers, the shift mainly requires a change of (7)____ rather than a single dramatic purchase. Instead of chasing every passing trend, thoughtful buyers deliberately choose (8)____ pieces that last for years and can be repaired rather than thrown away. Some even learn to mend a torn seam or creatively (9)____ an old jacket into something fresh and new. Critics still argue that sustainable clothing remains too (10)____ for many ordinary households, and that real change must ultimately come from government regulation, not individual choice. Supporters reply that small daily habits, multiplied across millions of wardrobes, can steadily reshape demand. Whatever the final truth, the conversation itself clearly signals a welcome new awareness. The clothes we choose to wear each morning quietly carry consequences that reach far beyond the simple moment we first put them on.
sets.explain_zh: (1)response 对策/回应，a serious ___ to that problem，名词，response to 搭配。(2)discard 丢弃，carelessly ___ garments，及物动词原形（与 buy 并列于 then 之后）。(3)fill 填满，mountains of waste now ___ landfills，复数主语动词原形。(4)limit 限制/减少，try to ___ this damage，原形动词。(5)harmful 有害的，far less ___ to rivers，形容词比较级，harmful to 搭配。(6)safe 安全的，ensuring ___ working conditions，形容词作定语。(7)mindset 心态，a change of ___，名词。(8)durable 耐用的，choose ___ pieces that last，形容词修饰 pieces。(9)transform 改造，___ an old jacket into，及物动词原形，transform into 搭配。(10)expensive 昂贵的，remains too ___ for households，形容词表语。干扰项：purchase(10) 文中已用 purchase 原词，bank 中无独立名词/动词槽；rapidly(11) 副词无修饰槽；ancient(12) 语义不合；honest(13) 无形容词槽；decline(14) 下降/婉拒，无匹配动词或名词槽。

sets.passage: Managing time well is far less about squeezing more tasks into a single day and far more about choosing what truly deserves our limited (1)____. Many people begin each morning with a long list and end it feeling completely exhausted yet strangely (2)____, having stayed busy without ever making any real progress. The basic trouble is that not all activities are equally (3)____; a few important ones quietly shape our entire future, while dozens of trivial ones merely consume precious hours. Effective planners gradually learn to (4)____ clearly between the merely urgent and the genuinely significant. They carefully protect long blocks of uninterrupted time and politely (5)____ requests that simply do not match their stated priorities. Saying no, they often discover, is actually the most productive skill of them all. Technology, meanwhile, can either greatly help or seriously (6)____ this whole effort. Used carelessly, constant notifications repeatedly (7)____ our fragile concentration and pull us back into shallow, distracted work. Used wisely, however, the very same tools can automate dull chores and free us for deeper thinking. Another common and costly mistake is ignoring proper rest. A tired mind works slowly and makes far more errors, so regular short breaks actually (8)____ our overall output. Perhaps the deepest lesson of all is that time, quite unlike money, can never truly be (9)____ once it has gone. A simple weekly review, where we honestly ask what worked and what merely wasted our hours, often reveals patterns we would otherwise miss entirely. Small adjustments, repeated steadily over many weeks, then quietly compound into surprisingly large results. By patiently aligning our small daily actions with our long-term (10)____, we slowly transform a frantic schedule into a calm and deliberate life.
sets.explain_zh: (1)attention 注意力，deserves our limited ___，名词。(2)unproductive 无成效的，exhausted yet strangely ___，形容词表语。(3)valuable 有价值的，equally ___，形容词表语。(4)distinguish 区分，learn to ___ between A and B，原形动词，distinguish between 搭配。(5)decline 婉拒，politely ___ requests，及物动词原形“拒绝”义。(6)harm 损害/妨碍，help or seriously ___ this effort，原形动词与 help 并列。(7)interrupt 打断，notifications repeatedly ___ our concentration，复数主语动词原形。(8)boost 提升，breaks actually ___ output，原形动词。(9)recovered 找回，can never be ___，被动语态过去分词。(10)goals 目标，long-term ___，名词复数。干扰项：expensive(10) 形容词无槽；rapidly(11) 副词无修饰槽；ancient(12) 语义不合；honest(13) 无形容词槽；purchase(14) 名词/动词无匹配槽。

sets.passage: There is a special and quiet pleasure in cooking at home that no busy restaurant can ever quite (1)____. The ordinary kitchen becomes a small theatre of the senses, where chopping, sizzling, and stirring slowly fill the warm air with (2)____ aromas. Unlike eating out, home cooking gives us almost complete (3)____ over exactly what goes into our own bodies. We alone decide how much salt to add, which oil to use, and whether to boldly (4)____ a recipe to suit our personal taste. This freedom naturally encourages experiment, and even a badly failed dish can teach a genuinely useful (5)____ for next time. Cooking also slows us down in a healthy and welcome way. Preparing a real meal from raw ingredients quietly demands both patience and steady (6)____, two qualities that the modern rush so often erodes. Many people surprisingly find the repetitive motions of peeling or kneading oddly (7)____, a gentle kind of meditation after a long and stressful day. Beyond the food itself, the shared act of cooking can powerfully strengthen the (8)____ between family members who happily share the work and later the crowded table. Children who regularly help in the kitchen tend to eat more vegetables and develop healthier habits that quietly (9)____ for the rest of their lives. Of course, time and energy are very real obstacles. A little planning helps greatly here, such as preparing a few basic ingredients in advance or cooking slightly larger portions to enjoy again the following day. Even simple dishes, made with care and shared with someone we love, can turn an ordinary evening into something quietly memorable. Yet even a single home-cooked meal each week can gently (10)____ a precious sense of comfort and control that processed dinners almost never provide.
sets.explain_zh: (1)match 比得上，no restaurant can quite ___，及物动词原形。(2)tempting 诱人的，___ aromas，形容词修饰 aromas。(3)control 掌控，complete ___ over what，名词，control over 搭配。(4)adapt 调整，boldly ___ a recipe，及物动词原形。(5)lesson 教训，teach a useful ___，名词。(6)focus 专注力，patience and steady ___，名词并列。(7)soothing 抚慰的，oddly ___，形容词表语。(8)bond 纽带，strengthen the ___ between，名词，bond between 搭配。(9)endure 持久存在，habits that quietly ___ for life，原形动词（先行词复数）。(10)restore 带来/恢复，can gently ___ a sense of comfort，及物动词原形。干扰项：expensive(10) 形容词无槽；rapidly(11) 副词无修饰槽；ancient(12) 语义不合；honest(13) 无形容词槽；purchase(14) 名词/动词无匹配槽。

sets.passage: Advertising now surrounds us so completely that we rarely even notice how deeply it quietly shapes our everyday desires. A truly clever campaign does not merely describe a product; instead it cleverly (1)____ a feeling, linking ordinary soap to happiness or a plain car to total freedom. Through endless repetition, these polished images gradually (2)____ our memory until a brand name strangely feels like an old and trusted friend. The most genuinely (3)____ advertisements rarely talk about price or quality at all. Instead, they carefully tell a story in which the viewer can vividly imagine a better, more (4)____ version of themselves. This emotional approach proves far more (5)____ than dry facts, because people clearly remember how a message made them feel long after they have completely forgotten its exact words. Critics worry that such clever persuasion can subtly (6)____ us to want expensive things we do not actually need, fuelling waste and personal debt. Defenders reply that advertising also usefully (7)____ important information and quietly supports the free media we all enjoy daily. Both views obviously contain some truth. What seems certain is that the average modern person now faces a constant daily (8)____ of commercial messages, and learning to read them critically has become an essential modern (9)____. When we finally pause to ask who created an image and what it quietly promises, the spell slowly begins to weaken, and we become noticeably more (10)____ of our own genuine wishes. Understanding advertising, in this sense, is not really about rejecting it entirely, but about quietly reclaiming a small measure of honest control over our own attention and money.
sets.explain_zh: (1)sells 兜售（情感），it cleverly ___ a feeling，第三人称单数动词。(2)shape 塑造，gradually ___ our memory，原形动词（情态 can 隐含于 gradually...结构，此处与前句并列叙述，主语 images 复数用原形）。(3)effective 有效的，the most genuinely ___ advertisements，最高级形容词修饰 advertisements。(4)confident 自信的，more ___ version of themselves，形容词。(5)memorable 难忘的，far more ___ than dry facts，形容词比较级。(6)persuade 说服，can subtly ___ us to want，原形动词，persuade sb to do。(7)provides 提供，advertising also usefully ___ information，第三人称单数动词。(8)flood 洪流，a constant daily ___ of messages，名词，flood of 搭配。(9)skill 技能，an essential modern ___，名词。(10)aware 意识到的，more ___ of our wishes，形容词，aware of 搭配。干扰项：expensive(10) 文中已用 expensive 原词，bank 中无独立形容词槽；rapidly(11) 副词无修饰槽；ancient(12) 语义不合；honest(13) 无形容词槽；purchase(14) 名词/动词无匹配槽。

sets.passage: Pursuing a personal hobby may look like a trivial use of precious free time, yet its real benefits reach surprisingly deep into our lives. When we fully (1)____ ourselves in an activity we genuinely love, whole hours can pass without notice, leaving us refreshed rather than completely drained. Psychologists describe this absorbed mental state as a powerful source of (2)____, quite distinct from the passive entertainment that leaves so many of us feeling oddly empty. A good hobby always offers a gentle (3)____: it stretches our abilities just enough to feel rewarding without ever becoming stressful or frightening. Over time, steady and patient practice builds real (4)____, and the quiet pride of slow improvement spills naturally over into other parts of life. Hobbies also warmly (5)____ us with other people who happen to share the very same passion, turning solitary interests into close communities. Whether the chosen pursuit is painting, gardening, or repairing old radios, it gives the busy mind a healthy place to (6)____ after the heavy pressures of work. Importantly, a hobby need never be (7)____ or even impressive to anyone else at all; its only true purpose is to bring quiet satisfaction to the person actually doing it. Many adults sadly abandon their interests, wrongly convinced that such simple pleasures are a (8)____ they can no longer afford. This is a real mistake. Even a few minutes of focused play can suddenly (9)____ fresh creativity and lift a tiring mood, making it among the most (10)____ investments we ever make.
sets.explain_zh: (1)immerse 沉浸，fully ___ ourselves in，及物动词原形，immerse oneself in 搭配。(2)joy 愉悦，a source of ___，名词。(3)challenge 挑战，a gentle ___，名词。(4)skill 技能，builds real ___，名词。(5)connect 连接，warmly ___ us with people，原形动词，connect sb with 搭配。(6)rest 休息，a place to ___，不定式原形动词。(7)useful 有用的，need never be ___ or impressive，形容词表语。(8)luxury 奢侈品，a ___ they can no longer afford，名词。(9)spark 激发，can suddenly ___ fresh creativity，及物动词原形。(10)valuable 有价值的，most ___ investments，最高级形容词修饰 investments。干扰项：expensive(10) 昂贵的，虽为形容词但不与 investments 自然搭配，且 (10) 语义需“有价值”；rapidly(11) 副词无修饰槽；ancient(12) 语义不合；honest(13) 无形容词槽；purchase(14) 名词/动词无匹配槽。

sets.passage: The modern coffee shop has quietly become one of the most important social spaces in the entire city. It is neither a private home nor a busy office, but rather a comfortable (1)____ ground where complete strangers can happily sit side by side for many hours. Sociologists often describe such welcoming places as truly essential to a healthy community, because they naturally (2)____ casual contact between people who might otherwise never meet at all. The warm smell of freshly roasting beans and the soft (3)____ of quiet conversation together create an atmosphere that feels both pleasantly lively and deeply (4)____. Some loyal customers come mainly to work, spreading out laptops and notebooks; others come simply to (5)____ the passing crowd through a large window. The friendly staff often (6)____ familiar regulars warmly by name, adding a small but genuinely precious touch of belonging to an otherwise (7)____ urban day. Of course, the whole experience is carefully designed for profit. Lighting, music, and seating are all deliberately chosen to gently (8)____ visitors to stay a little longer and order a little more. Yet even this gentle commerce still serves a very real human (9)____, offering quiet shelter and easy company to the lonely and the tired. For students, freelancers, and weary travellers alike, a familiar café can honestly feel like a warm second living room. Regulars often develop their own quiet routines, claiming a favourite corner table and ordering the very same drink week after week without a single word. Over months, these tiny habits slowly knit complete strangers into a loose but genuine little community. In our screen-filled age, these small fragrant rooms remind us how much we still deeply (10)____ the simple comfort of being among others.
sets.explain_zh: (1)neutral 中立的，a comfortable ___ ground，形容词，neutral ground 中立地带。(2)encourage 促进，they naturally ___ casual contact，原形动词（先行词 places 复数）。(3)hum 低语/嗡嗡声，the soft ___ of conversation，名词。(4)relaxing 令人放松的，lively and deeply ___，形容词表语。(5)watch 观看，come simply to ___ the crowd，原形动词。(6)greet 招呼，staff often ___ regulars by name，原形动词。(7)anonymous 无名的/匿名的，an otherwise ___ urban day，形容词修饰 day。(8)tempt 诱使，chosen to gently ___ visitors to stay，原形动词，tempt sb to do。(9)need 需求，a very real human ___，名词。(10)value 珍视，how much we still deeply ___，原形动词。干扰项：expensive(10) 形容词无槽；rapidly(11) 副词无修饰槽；ancient(12) 语义不合；honest(13) 无形容词槽；purchase(14) 名词/动词无匹配槽。

sets.passage: In a world that prizes raw speed above almost everything else, the simple decision to slow right down can feel like a quiet act of (1)____. We rush through our meals, our conversations, and even our supposedly relaxing holidays, firmly convinced that being constantly busy is the surest sign of a (2)____ life. Yet careful research increasingly suggests the very opposite is true: a permanently frantic pace tends to seriously (3)____ both our long-term health and our daily happiness. When the restless mind never pauses to rest, stress hormones quietly (4)____ in the tired body, and small ordinary worries swell into much larger ones. Choosing to slow down certainly does not mean becoming lazy or completely (5)____ our deepest ambition. It simply means giving each separate task the full (6)____ it honestly deserves, so that we actually experience our own lives rather than merely surviving them. People who patiently practise this gentle art slowly learn to savour ordinary moments, carefully noticing the (7)____ details that a hurried mind always ignores. They calmly walk instead of run, and they politely listen instead of (8)____ others mid-sentence. Such unusual habits clearly require real (9)____, because the surrounding culture constantly pushes us to do more, ever faster. But the reward is a far deeper sense of presence and calm. Small daily rituals help enormously here, whether a slow morning coffee enjoyed without a phone or a short evening walk taken with no particular destination in mind. These quiet pauses gradually retrain a restless mind to settle. Ironically, working at a gentler, more sustainable speed often makes us noticeably more (10)____ in the long run, since well-rested minds think more clearly and make far fewer careless mistakes.
sets.explain_zh: (1)rebellion 反叛，a quiet act of ___，名词。(2)successful 成功的，a ___ life，形容词修饰 life。(3)damage 损害，tends to seriously ___ our health，原形动词。(4)build 累积，stress hormones quietly ___ in the body，复数主语动词原形（积聚义）。(5)abandoning 放弃，becoming lazy or completely ___ ambition，动名词与 becoming 并列后接宾语 ambition。(6)attention 关注/精力，the full ___ it deserves，名词。(7)subtle 微妙的，the ___ details，形容词修饰 details。(8)interrupting 打断，listen instead of ___ others，介词 of 后接动名词。(9)courage 勇气，require real ___，名词。(10)productive 高效的，more ___ in the long run，形容词比较级表语。干扰项：expensive(10) 形容词无匹配槽；rapidly(11) 副词无修饰槽；ancient(12) 语义不合；honest(13) 文中已用 honestly 副词，bank 的 honest 无形容词槽；purchase(14) 名词/动词无匹配槽。
